mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of instances following the trigger, or rolling Rank IC remains negative across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy aggressively, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint high growth and inflation periods yield positive forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and healthy supply-side capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with rising INDPRO, or autocorrelation dominates the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding consumer spending capacity and robust aggregate demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales indicates immediate weakness in consumer demand, which reduces near-term corporate cash flows and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines are followed by positive equity returns in >60% of cases, or signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated nominal interest rates combined with persistent consumer price inflation compress real returns and increase discount rates, which systematically reduces equity valuations and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign during tightening periods, or forward returns remain positive despite high real rates.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of constrained capacity utilization signals supply-side tightening that supports pricing power and margin expansion, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of supply-constrained periods, or the signal is fully subsumed by monetary policy variables.
